seo.title: DraftKings Withdrawal Times & Limits (2026)
seo.description: Learn the fastest ways to withdraw from DraftKings. Breakdown of PayPal, Bank Transfer, and Debit Card limits, times, and rules.

tax_reporting.notes: DraftKings is legally required to issue W-2G forms for wins exceeding $600 or 300:1 odds. All wins are taxable regardless of form issuance.

withdrawal_methods.pro_tip: DraftKings enforces a 'Closed-Loop' policy. You MUST have deposited with this exact debit card previously for it to be eligible for withdrawals.
Ensure the name on your PayPal account perfectly matches your DraftKings registered name to avoid manual review holds.
DraftKings does not permit withdrawals to business banking accounts. Personal accounts only.

verification.pro_tip: Make sure your registration address matches your public records and banking documents perfectly.

common_delays: Large wins triggering a manual W-2G tax review
Attempting to withdraw to a method not used for deposit
withdrawal_experience.rollover_rules: Deposits must be played through 1x. Bonus bets only pay out the profit, not the initial stake.
withdrawal_experience.cancellation_window: Very short. Can cancel pending withdrawals via the withdrawal tracker UI.

faqs.question: How fast are DraftKings withdrawals?
faqs.answer: Debit card and PayPal withdrawals typically process in under 24 hours, and often within an hour. Bank wires take up to 2 days, and physical checks can take up to 14 days.

faqs.question: Are there fees for withdrawing from DraftKings?
faqs.answer: No, DraftKings does not charge fees for standard withdrawal methods like PayPal, debit cards, or online banking.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw my bonus bets on DraftKings?
faqs.answer: No. Bonus bets cannot be withdrawn directly. You must wager them, and any profit you make becomes withdrawable cash.
